context: Tests whether the agent uses Declarative pipeline syntax (not scripted), retrieves credentials from the Jenkins Credentials Store rather than pipeline parameters, includes workspace cleanup in post, and adds standard pipeline options.

checklist.name: Declarative syntax
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile starts with 'pipeline {' (Declarative syntax) — NOT 'node {' (Scripted syntax)

checklist.name: No credential parameters
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile does NOT contain a parameters block with string or password entries for credentials (API keys, passwords, tokens)

checklist.name: Credentials Store used
checklist.description: Registry credentials are accessed via credentials() binding in an environment block OR via withCredentials([...]) — NOT hardcoded strings

checklist.name: cleanWs in post
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile includes a post block with an always { cleanWs() } or always { deleteDir() } call

checklist.name: post block exists
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile contains a post { } block at the pipeline level (not just inside a stage)

checklist.name: timeout option
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile options block includes a timeout() directive

checklist.name: buildDiscarder option
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile options block includes a buildDiscarder(logRotator(...)) directive

checklist.name: timestamps option
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile options block includes a timestamps() directive

checklist.name: Stages present
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile contains at least 3 named stages covering build, test, and a deployment-related step

checklist.name: No hardcoded secret values
checklist.description: No literal password, token, or secret string appears in the Jenkinsfile body outside of a credentialsId reference
